overall_assessment: The corrected RR-AUTH-04 suite is mostly real-backend and behavior-oriented, but it is not fully closure-grade yet because the documented reissue-after-post-consume-failure reset contract is not directly asserted and the slice still relies on disclosed brownfield regression provenance rather than preserved fail-first evidence.
recommended_path: Add a focused regression that forces password persistence to fail after token consumption in the reset flow so the documented burn-and-reissue contract is proven, then carry the existing brownfield/TDD debt forward explicitly as non-hidden residual risk.

findings.title: The documented post-consume failure contract is not directly regression-tested
findings.rationale: RR-AUTH-04 explicitly documents that once a reset token is consumed, any later password-mutation failure must leave the token burned and require reissue (`foundation_documentation/artifacts/post-release-public-auth-password-reset-risk-hardening-package.md:149-150`, `foundation_documentation/todos/active/post_release_hardening/TODO-post-release-public-auth-password-reset-and-risk-matrix-hardening.md:148,177`). The code implements that ordering by consuming before save in both tenant and landlord paths (`laravel-app/app/Application/Profiles/TenantProfileService.php:130-139`, `laravel-app/app/Application/Profiles/LandlordProfileService.php:82-88`), but the current tests only cover happy-path reset, stale-token rejection, and token deletion (`laravel-app/tests/Unit/Application/Auth/PasswordResetTokenServiceTest.php:62-124`, `laravel-app/tests/Feature/Tenants/PasswordRegistrationControllerTest.php:241-317`, `laravel-app/tests/Api/v1/Admin/ApiV1AdminProfileTest.php:33-62,78-113`, `laravel-app/tests/Unit/Application/Profiles/LandlordProfileServiceTest.php:65-89`). A future refactor could weaken that burn-before-mutate recovery rule without tripping this suite.
findings.suggested_action: Add a focused regression that injects a post-consume password-save failure and then proves the just-consumed token cannot be replayed in both tenant and landlord reset paths, or at the shared abstraction level if the failure mode is centralized.

findings.title: Fail-first evidence remains disclosed debt rather than preserved proof for this bugfix slice
findings.rationale: The authority packet explicitly says RR-AUTH-04 was normalized after code/test hardening had already started, so preserved fail-first/red-run artifacts are unavailable (`foundation_documentation/todos/active/post_release_hardening/TODO-post-release-public-auth-password-reset-and-risk-matrix-hardening.md:110-112,180-181`, `foundation_documentation/artifacts/post-release-public-auth-password-reset-risk-hardening-package.md:142-143`). The current regression assertions look meaningful and real-backend, so this is not a hidden coverage failure, but it does leave TDD alignment weaker than ideal for a release-critical auth hardening slice.
findings.suggested_action: Keep this debt explicit in the acceptance record and require preserved red-run or equivalent bounded failing-assertion evidence on the next auth/security bugfix slice instead of treating retrofit coverage as the default.
